refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational policies, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a versioned history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked specificity on traceability, history management, and safety-critical aspects. Refinement adds: (1) explicit linkage to worksite mission context and safety policies, (2) baseline recording with traceability, (3) versioned history with audit trail, (4) safety margin derivation as a distinct performance target. This aligns with acceptance criteria and high safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically derived from documented worksite inputs (mission, policies, constraints), (b) each target is traceable to source worksite data, (c) baseline snapshots are recorded with timestamp and operator identity, (d) all updates are logged with reason and approval chain, (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and parse worksite mission context (site layout, task sequence, environmental constraints, operational policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using documented algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record performance baseline with full traceability to source worksite inputs and timestamp.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain versioned history of all performance requirement updates with audit trail (who, when, why, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Validate derived performance targets against worksite safety plan and operational policies before activation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission data, applies requirement derivation algorithms, records baselines to a versioned database, and maintains audit logs. Interfaces with Telemetry (inbound mission context) and Safety Services (outbound safety margin validation).

DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules independently, with periodic synchronization to a central baseline store. Higher diffusion, lower cohesion.

DP-C: Manual Derivation with Template Library – Site Manager manually selects performance templates from a library, with system recording and versioning. Lower automation, higher human error risk, unsuitable for high safety criticality.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation Engine – Centralized engine for baseline derivation and validation, with local caching in Vehicle and Teleoperation for real-time performance monitoring. Balances centralization with operational responsiveness.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with integrated audit logging and safety validation.

potential_sources: Coupling to Telemetry interface (mission context inbound) is necessary and well-defined.
Coupling to Safety Services (safety margin validation outbound) is necessary and traceable to high safety criticality.
No unnecessary coupling to Vehicle or Teleoperation internals; interfaces are clean.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to other functional domains. The derivation engine is logically independent; it consumes standardized telemetry and produces standardized safety alerts. This supports modularity and testability.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured, parseable format (e.g., JSON, XML) at system startup and during operational updates.
ASSUMPTION: Requirement derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be formally specified and validated.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or malformed, derivation may fail or produce invalid targets. Mitigation: Implement schema validation and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: Audit trail storage may grow unbounded over system lifetime. Mitigation: Implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: Safety margin derivation logic may be domain-specific and difficult to generalize. Mitigation: Engage Safety Manager early in algorithm design; validate against worksite safety plan.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the source and format of worksite mission context (e.g., is it provided by Site Manager via UI, ingested from external planning system, or derived from sensor telemetry?). Define the set of requirement derivation algorithms and their validation criteria. Specify audit trail retention and archival policy.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Safety Services module shall own the Performance Derivation Engine, including: (1) ingestion of worksite mission context and operational policies, (2) execution of requirement derivation algorithms, (3) recording and versioning of performance baselines, (4) maintenance of audit trail, (5) validation of derived targets against safety plan, (6) generation of SafetyAlert notifications for out-of-policy targets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Worksite mission context (site layout, task sequence, environmental constraints, operational policies, current vehicle state). Format: structured telemetry payload with mission metadata.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outbound: Safety validation results, performance baseline confirmations, and alerts for derived targets that violate worksite safety plan or operational policies. Includes audit trail entries.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Operational task updates and policy changes from Site Manager or Tele-operator that trigger re-derivation of performance targets.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Assigning IM here would diffuse ownership and make the module a catch-all. Not recommended for a cohesive, testable component.

Vehicle module is responsible for vehicle-specific control and state. Performance derivation is a system-level concern, not vehicle-specific. High diffusion risk; would couple vehicle logic to mission planning.
Teleoperation module handles remote operator interface and command relay. Performance derivation is orthogonal to teleoperation; assigning here would conflate concerns and reduce reusability.

Safety Services is the natural home for performance requirement derivation and validation. High safety criticality, audit trail, and policy validation are core safety concerns. Cohesive ownership, minimal diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services module is the optimal container for the Performance Derivation Engine because: (1) requirement derivation is inherently a safety-critical function (high safety criticality tailoring factor), (2) validation against worksite safety plan is a safety concern, (3) audit trail and traceability are safety governance requirements, (4) Safety Services already owns policy validation and alert generation, (5) this assignment minimizes diffusion and maintains cohesion.
containment_risks: RISK: If Safety Services becomes overloaded with non-safety functions, the module may lose focus. Mitigation: Clearly scope Safety Services to safety-critical derivation and validation; defer non-critical performance monitoring to Vehicle or Teleoperation.

RISK: Performance derivation may require real-time responsiveness that conflicts with Safety Services' safety-critical timing constraints. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous derivation with cached results; validate timing requirements early.

RISK: Worksite mission context may be generated by Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, creating circular dependencies. Mitigation: Establish clear data flow: Vehicle/Teleoperation produce telemetry → Safety Services consumes and derives → results flow back via SafetyAlert.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Safety Services has capacity and expertise to own performance derivation. Define the interface contract for Telemetry (mission context format) and SafetyAlert (validation results format) in detail. Establish a clear data flow diagram showing how mission context flows from Vehicle/Teleoperation to Safety Services and how derived targets flow back.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all refined FR requirements: (1) derives targets from mission context, (2) records baselines with traceability, (3) maintains versioned history with audit trail, (4) validates against safety policies. The DP is specific, implementable, and traceable to FR sub-requirements. No ambiguity or missing mappings.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: Safety Services assignment is cohesive and minimizes diffusion. No evidence of logic spreading across multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services containment score is 0.95 (excellent). The module boundary is clear: Safety Services owns derivation engine, audit trail, and validation logic. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) are well-defined and non-invasive. Ownership is unambiguous.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH: Performance derivation engine can be unit-tested independently. Baseline recording and audit trail can be verified against test mission contexts. Safety validation can be tested against known policy violations. Traceability can be verified via audit log inspection.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR: All refined FR sub-requirements are allocated to Safety Services via the Performance Derivation Engine. Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces provide clear input/output boundaries. Audit trail provides traceability evidence.

recommended_revisions: Define the exact format and schema for worksite mission context (Telemetry inbound payload).
Specify the requirement derivation algorithms and their validation criteria (e.g., cycle time calculation formula, energy efficiency model).
Document the audit trail schema and retention policy.
Establish acceptance criteria for performance baseline recording (e.g., timestamp precision, operator identity capture).
Define the safety policy validation rules and how they are encoded in the Safety Services module.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-formed and decomposable into five atomic sub-FRs. DP-A is sound and exhibits low coupling. Safety Services is the optimal IM with high containment and low diffusion. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. The next level of decomposition is warranted to detail the requirement derivation algorithms, audit trail implementation, and safety validation logic. Proceeding to sub-FR decomposition will increase design clarity and reduce implementation risk.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and parse worksite mission context (site layout, task sequence, environmental constraints, operational policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using documented algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record performance baseline with full traceability to source worksite inputs and timestamp.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain versioned history of all performance requirement updates with audit trail (who, when, why, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Validate derived performance targets against worksite safety plan and operational policies before activation.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured, parseable format at system startup and during operational updates.
Requirement derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be formally specified and validated.
Safety Services module has capacity and expertise to own performance derivation and audit trail management.
Telemetry interface can reliably deliver mission context to Safety Services without loss or corruption.
SafetyAlert interface can reliably deliver validation results and audit trail entries to stakeholders.
open_risks: Mission context may be incomplete or malformed, causing derivation failure. Mitigation: Implement schema validation and fallback to conservative defaults.
Audit trail storage may grow unbounded. Mitigation: Implement retention policy and archival strategy.
Safety margin derivation logic may be domain-specific and difficult to generalize. Mitigation: Engage Safety Manager early in algorithm design.
Performance derivation may require real-time responsiveness that conflicts with Safety Services' safety-critical timing constraints. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous derivation with cached results.
Circular dependencies may arise if Vehicle/Teleoperation generate mission context. Mitigation: Establish clear data flow and interface contracts.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and example payloads (from Site Manager or planning system).
Requirement derivation algorithm specifications (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margin formulas).
Worksite safety plan and operational policy documents (to inform validation rules).
Audit trail schema and retention policy (from Maintenance/Quality and System Engineer).
Performance baseline acceptance criteria and traceability requirements (from Project Manager and System Engineer).
Safety Services module capacity assessment and timing constraints (from System Engineer).
